title: Inconsistent behaviour when building images with and without --platform parameter
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to build 2 images. Image A and Image B. Image B is based on Image A.

If I build **Image A** on a machine and don't specify the platform explicitly, then **Image A** is put to local container storage, and can be referenced in **Image B**'s `Containerfile`'s FROM argument. Building **Image B** correctly uses the previously built **Image A** from local container storage.

If, instead, I build **Image A**, but include the `--platform` parameter, and use the platform of the current build machine, then when I reference the **Image A** in Image B's `Containerfile`, then it will try and pull that image from the remote repository rather than using the one stored in the local container storage.

This behaviour makes our multiplatform builds incredibly complicated, as I have to:

- build **Image A** without the platform tag - giving it **Tag A**
- run local pipeline tests (which reference the newly built image using the generic **Tag A**)
- if successful then rebuild **Image A** all over again using the platform parameter & manifest (that replaces **Tag A**)
- push the architecture-specific images & manifest to the remote repository
- finally, rebuild the original non-platform-parameter image with **Tag A** in order that I can use the image in subsequent tests further down the build pipeline on the build agent

It seems crazy to have to rebuild the same image 3 times for the same platform


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Given `A.Containerfile`:

```
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM docker.io/alpine:latest
LABEL description="Image A"
```

and `B.Containerfile`:

```
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM docker.io/test-container:1.0
LABEL description="Image B"
```

2. When I build Image A:
```
podman build -f A.Containerfile  --tag docker.io/test-container:1.0 .      
STEP 1/2: FROM --platform=linux/arm64/v8 docker.io/alpine:latest
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:6e771e15690e2fabf2332d3a3b744495411d6e0b00b2aea64419b58b0066cf81
Copying config sha256:8d591b0b7dea080ea3be9e12ae563eebf9869168ffced1cb25b2470a3d9fe15e
Writing manifest to image destination
STEP 2/2: LABEL description="Image A"
COMMIT docker.io/test-container:1.0
--> b6d24270e20f
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/test-container:1.0
b6d24270e20f8a54c8912f945b1e40f84bc0671aedd4d2843c0d2f60e3698a8b
```

And I build Image B:

```
podman build -f B.Containerfile  --tag docker.io/test-container-2:1.0 .
STEP 1/2: FROM --platform=linux/arm64/v8 docker.io/test-container:1.0
STEP 2/2: LABEL description="Image B"
COMMIT docker.io/test-container-2:1.0
--> a56b5910e000
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/test-container-2:1.0
a56b5910e000af1c969b69f597ce1849fac70977b2d4d2cfffc75cb9983c9b35
```
3. Then it succeeds.
4. However, when I build Image A with the `--platform` parameter:
```
podman build -f A.Containerfile --platform=linux/amd64 --tag docker.io/test-container:1.0 .
STEP 1/2: FROM --platform=linux/arm64/v8 docker.io/alpine:latest
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:6e771e15690e2fabf2332d3a3b744495411d6e0b00b2aea64419b58b0066cf81
Copying config sha256:8d591b0b7dea080ea3be9e12ae563eebf9869168ffced1cb25b2470a3d9fe15e
Writing manifest to image destination
STEP 2/2: LABEL description="Image A"
COMMIT docker.io/test-container:1.0
--> 08956c55769a
Successfully tagged docker.io/library/test-container:1.0
08956c55769a38abbab47e2cf141b81eba7fb00c0e6b826bf592e21cd9bf485c
```

And I build Image B with the `--platform` parameter:

```
 podman build -f B.Containerfile  --platform=linux/amd64 --tag docker.io/test-container-2:1.0 .
STEP 1/2: FROM --platform=linux/arm64/v8 docker.io/test-container:1.0
Trying to pull docker.io/library/test-container:1.0...
Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://test-container:1.0: reading manifest 1.0 in docker.io/library/test-container: requested access to the resource is denied

```

5. Then it fails to use the locally built image

I've also tried without using the `--platform=$BUILDPLATFORM` within the `FROM` argument




### Describe the results you received

Podman always tries to download from the remote repository
```
Trying to pull docker.io/library/test-container:1.0...
Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://test-container:1.0: reading manifest 1.0 in docker.io/library/test-container: requested access to the resource is denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

I would expect `FROM` to use the locally built image when `--platform` parameter is passed to `podman build`

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

I see various other issues that may be related, such as #23055, #17237 and #18896 - but they aren't quite the same. If I don't use the `--platform` parameter to Podman, then things appear to behave themselves.

comments.body: I notice that my example was building an amd64 image on an arm64 build agent. It appears OK if my platform and build target match. I just need to determine how to handle the tagging issue now (using the same tag for locally build images and the pushed multi-platform manifest).
